retired.producer: removed Cloud HTTP action and request/mapper chain
retired.consumerEvidence: Cross-repository scan found no Edge production invocation of GetProcessUploadPath; Cloud callers were tests only.
retired.reason: No production caller existed in Cloud or Edge; all remaining test callers were migrated to the typed pass-station batch contract.
retired.deletionCondition: Delete when producer-to-real-consumer scan is zero and canonical success, duplicate and authorization coverage is present; satisfied in this batch.

retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.decisionReason: The endpoint is the current typed selector API consumed by Cloud Web dictionaries and forms; it does not adapt an older contract.
retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.deletionCondition: Delete only with a contract migration of every typed Web consumer.

retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.decisionReason: The endpoint is the current active-device selector consumed by recipes, employees and pass-station UI flows.
retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.deletionCondition: Delete only with a contract migration of every typed Web consumer.

retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.decisionReason: BlockedAliases is negative security configuration consumed by GatewayRouteCatalog; it exposes no alias route or compatibility producer.
retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.deletionCondition: Delete only when an equivalent mandatory route-denial control replaces the catalog.

retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.decisionReason: HostApi and plugin-version compatibility is the current release-domain acceptance rule used by package generation and inventory diagnostics, not an adapter for a retired API.
retainedOrdinaryAbstractions.deletionCondition: Delete only if the host/plugin version contract itself is retired and all release consumers migrate.

retainedCompatibility.decisionReason: This is genuine compatibility logic for two current Edge producers, not an assumed older caller: CapacitySyncTask.CreatePayload and DeviceLogSyncTask.PostLogsAsync omit RequestId today. Pass-station upload sends RequestId and does not depend on this fallback. The legacy-prefixed content hash therefore preserves current capacity/device-log persisted DeduplicationKey semantics until those real producers and stored keys migrate.
retainedCompatibility.replacement: RequestId-required capacity and device-log Edge upload contracts with a one-way persisted-key migration; pass-station already uses RequestId
retainedCompatibility.deletionCondition: Add mandatory RequestId/idempotency-key generation to the real Edge CapacitySyncTask and DeviceLogSyncTask producers, deploy that contract across the supported fleet, inventory and migrate persisted legacy-prefixed DeduplicationKey rows, and prove both transitional no-RequestId behavior and the final RequestId-only path before physical deletion.

retainedCompatibility.decisionReason: This is genuine compatibility logic. MigrationWorkApp Worker resolves the orchestrator at startup, InitializeAsync invokes the schema checks, and the job runs before dependent services; removal without a fleet/database baseline would break upgrades.
retainedCompatibility.replacement: EF migrations or a one-way schema migration covering all three compatibility checks
retainedCompatibility.deletionCondition: Inventory every deployed database at the post-normalization schema baseline, replace ad-hoc checks with irreversible migrations, and prove clean-install plus upgrade-path tests before physical deletion.
